name: Self-interaction cross-section limits from cluster mergers
requirement: Any candidate framework predicting self-interacting dark matter must respect upper limits on the self-interaction cross section sigma_DM/m from cluster-merger systems (Bullet, Abell 520, MACS J0025, ...). Harvey et al. 2015 stack 30 cluster mergers and find sigma_DM/m < 0.47 cm^2/g (95% CL) at velocities ~1000 km/s.

regime.description: Galaxy-cluster-scale DM with internal velocities ~1000 km/s; mergers shock baryonic gas while DM passes nearly collisionlessly.

current_bound.units: cm^2/g (95% CL upper limit) at v ~ 1000 km/s
current_bound.experiment: Stacked weak-lensing analysis of cluster mergers
current_bound.reference: Harvey, Massey, Kitching, Taylor, Tittley, 'The non-gravitational interactions of dark matter in colliding galaxy clusters', Science 347, 1462 (2015), arXiv:1503.07675

what_it_excludes: Constrains self-interacting dark matter (SIDM) models proposed to alleviate small-scale CDM problems (cusp-core, missing satellites, too-big-to-fail). Velocity-dependent SIDM models with sigma/m ~ 1-10 cm^2/g at dwarf-galaxy scales (v ~ 100 km/s) but ~0.1 cm^2/g at cluster scales remain viable.

parameterization.name: Self-interaction cross section sigma_DM(v) / m as a function of relative velocity. Velocity-dependent SIDM (Yukawa mediator) parameterized by mediator mass and coupling.
parameterization.reference: Tulin and Yu, Phys. Rep. 730, 1 (2018); Spergel and Steinhardt, PRL 84, 3760 (2000) (original SIDM proposal).
parameterization.summary: Cluster-merger constraint: sigma/m < 0.47 cm^2/g at v ~ 1000 km/s. Dwarf-galaxy halo cores prefer sigma/m ~ 1 cm^2/g at v ~ 30-100 km/s.

procedural.predicted_observable: Spatial offset of DM mass relative to galaxy positions in merging clusters; mass loss from interactions.
framework_inputs: If the framework includes a self-interacting DM sector, compute the elastic scattering cross section sigma(v) including the velocity dependence from any light mediator.

primary_references.citation: Harvey et al., 'The non-gravitational interactions of dark matter in colliding galaxy clusters', Science 347, 1462 (2015)
Tulin and Yu, 'Dark matter self-interactions and small scale structure', Phys. Rep. 730, 1 (2018)
